title: Needs vs. Wants
summary: Learn to tell the difference so your money goes where it matters.

cards.prompt: Every dollar you spend is either a need or a want.
cards.explanation: Nail this one distinction and budgeting gets a lot easier. Let's break it down with stuff you actually deal with.

cards.prompt: You just got your first paycheck from your part-time job. Which of these is a NEED?
options: Gas money to get to your shift
cards.explanation: You need to physically get to work to keep earning money. No gas, no paycheck.

cards.prompt: True or false: if something makes your life easier, it automatically counts as a need.
cards.explanation: Lots of things make life easier without being needs. A need is something you can't reasonably do without — food, shelter, transportation to work or school, basic health care.
A working phone is how school, work, and your parents reach you in an emergency. The $1,200 phone upgrade is a different story.

cards.prompt: You already have working wireless earbuds. New AirPods Pro would be a...
cards.explanation: Same category of item, different situation. You already have a working version — this is an upgrade, which makes it a want.

cards.prompt: The trick: needs keep you fed, housed, safe, healthy, or able to work and go to school.
cards.explanation: Everything else — even stuff you use every day, even stuff that feels essential in the moment — is a want. Wants aren't bad. You just budget for them after needs.

cards.prompt: True or false: car insurance is a want if you only drive occasionally.
cards.explanation: If you're driving at all, insurance is legally required in almost every state and covers you if something goes wrong. That makes it a need, no matter how often you drive.

cards.prompt: Say you take home $200 from a weekend of work. $130 has to go to actual needs (gas, phone bill, saving toward your car payment). How many dollars are left over for wants?
cards.explanation: $200 − $130 = $70. That's the number you actually get to have fun with — everything else was already spoken for.
